reasoning: A country developing or acquiring a nuclear weapon involves substantial time, resources, political shifts, and usually withdrawal from the NPT (which requires 90 days' notice). Currently, no eligible US ally (NATO member or Major Non-NATO Ally as of March 2025) without nuclear weapons has an active, disclosed nuclear weapons program. While countries like South Korea and Japan possess advanced nuclear technology, starting a weapons program and achieving an operational device by December 2025 is functionally impossible given the timeframe. An outright transfer of independent control of US nuclear weapons to an ally would violate the NPT (Article I), facing insurmountable domestic, international, and logistical roadblocks before the end of 2025. Israel and Pakistan already possess nuclear weapons and are therefore excluded by the criteria, while Ukraine is neither a NATO member nor an MNNA. Thus, the probability is virtually zero.
